text: Good API design is essential for creating services that developers love to use. When designing REST APIs, choose clear and intuitive endpoints that represent resources, like `/users` or `/orders`. Use appropriate status codes to communicate results: `200` for success, `201` for created resources, and `404` for not found. Always include versioning in your API, for example `/v1/users`, so that changes don’t break existing users. Finally, write thorough, accessible documentation that explains endpoints, parameters, and response examples. If developers can understand and integrate your API without frustration, you’ve done a great job.
